content.problemStatement:
Masha has NNN minions. Each minion has two characteristics: power aaa and endurance bbb. Masha thinks that a non-empty set of minions {m1,m2,‚Ä¶,mk}\{m_1, m_2, \dots, m_k\}{m1‚Äã,m2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,mk‚Äã} with characteristics (am1,bm1),(am2,bm2),‚Ä¶,(amk,bmk)(a_{m_1},b_{m_1}), (a_{m_2},b_{m_2}), \dots, (a_{m_k},b_{m_k})(am1‚Äã‚Äã,bm1‚Äã‚Äã),(am2‚Äã‚Äã,bm2‚Äã‚Äã),‚Ä¶,(amk‚Äã‚Äã,bmk‚Äã‚Äã) is good if the mean endurance of this set doesn't exceed the minimum power in it, i.e. if min(am1,am2,‚Ä¶,amk)‚â•(bm1+bm2+‚ãØ+bmk)/kmin(a_{m_1}, a_{m_2}, \dots, a_{m_k}) \ge (b_{m_1}+b_{m_2}+\dots+b_{m_k}) / kmin(am1‚Äã‚Äã,am2‚Äã‚Äã,‚Ä¶,amk‚Äã‚Äã)‚â•(bm1‚Äã‚Äã+bm2‚Äã‚Äã+‚ãØ+bmk‚Äã‚Äã)/k.

Masha would like to choose a good subset of her minions and give these minions to Mark. Your task is to calculate the maximum number of minions Masha can give to Mark.
constraints: the sum of NNN for all test cases does not exceed 4‚ãÖ1054\cdot10^54‚ãÖ105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The following NNN lines describe minions. Each of these lines contains two space-separated integers aaa and bbb denoting the power and endurance of one minion.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one number ‚Äî the size of the largest good set of minions, or 000 if no such set exists.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The set of minions {2,3}\{2, 3\}{2,3} is good, since min(3,2)‚â•(3+1)/2\mathrm{min}(3,2) \ge (3+1)/2min(3,2)‚â•(3+1)/2.
Example case 2: There is no non-empty good subset of minions.
